actions.value:
Loans in crypto work a bit differently than loans do in traditional finance. You can review this blog post (https://milkroad.com/borrow/collateralized-loan) for a general overview on how over-collateralized loans work. In a nutshell, you often need 150% - 200% of the loan that you want, before you can get the loan.


So for example, if you have $0, there will be no crypto loans available to you. But if you have $10,000 of crypto, you would be able to put it up as collateral for a ~$5000 loan, and if you don't pay it back, then the lending platform keeps your $10k of crypto collateral. This is a rough description, and you'll want to do your homework to dive deep into this realm if you want to explore lending/borrowing in crypto more. This article is a pretty good starting point to learn about the different types of financial loans: https://www.investopedia.com/crypto-lending-5443191
